name: Operations
description: Your full-stack AI operations operator for solo founders. One agent covering planning, scheduling, finance hygiene, vendors, and data. No upfront onboarding — I ask for what I need as we work. Chat-first, markdown outputs, drafts only (I never send, close, or commit).
author: Houston Simplified Verticals

agentSeeds..houston/activity.json: [{"id": "a1c7f4b8-3e9d-4c2f-b6a1-08c5d2e1f7b3", "title": "Start anywhere \u2014 I'll ask for what I need", "description": "No upfront onboarding. Open the Overview tab and click any tile that sounds useful \u2014 morning brief, Monday review, SaaS audit, renewal calendar, experiment analysis, OKR refresh. When I need something specific about your company, priorities, VIPs, vendor posture, or warehouse connection, I'll ask one targeted question (best way first: connect via Composio in the Integrations tab) and remember it.", "status": "needs_you"}]
agentSeeds..houston/routines/routines.json:
[{"id": "weekly-ops-review", "name": "Monday operations review", "description": "Every Monday morning: what shipped last week, what's stale, what's next — rolled up across Planning, Scheduling, Finance, Vendors, and Data.", "prompt": "Run the Monday operations review. Use the run-review skill with period=weekly.

First, if the latest rollups/ file is older than 7 days, also run run-review with period=metrics-rollup and fold its top movers into the review.

Aggregate everything this agent produced in the last 7 days from outputs.json. Group by domain (Planning / Scheduling / Finance / Vendors / Data). For each domain, surface (a) what shipped, (b) what's stale — started but not touched in 3+ weeks, (c) the single most useful next move.

Cross-reference with active priorities from context/operations-context.md (honest verdict per priority: on-track / at-risk / off-track) and the 30-day renewal window from renewals/calendar.md. Flag any investor-update or board deadline coming up this week.

End with THE ONE MOVE — a single most-useful thing to do this week.

Write to reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log to outputs.json. This routine always surfaces — the review matters even on a quiet week.", "schedule": "0 8 * * 1", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "weekly-followup-sweep", "name": "Weekly follow-up sweep", "description": "Every Friday afternoon: what did I commit to that's due or overdue? Drafts the fulfillment or an honest bump so Monday doesn't start with debt.", "prompt": "Run the weekly follow-up sweep. Use the draft-message skill with type=followup (HANDLE sub-mode).

Read followups.json. For every follow-up with status='pending' and dueAt <= today + 3 days, either (a) flip to 'ready-to-close' if there's an addressing outbound in drafts/ already, or (b) draft either the fulfillment or an honest bump in my voice ('Following up on the {X} I promised by {Y} — status: {Z}'). Save each draft to drafts/followup-{date}-{slug}.md AND as an inbox draft.

Summarize to me: count of newly-drafted followups, count of ready-to-close, count still genuinely-pending-with-date.

Always surface — follow-up debt never goes away on its own.", "schedule": "0 15 * * 5", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-renewal-saas-scan", "name": "Monthly renewals + SaaS sweep", "description": "1st of every month: refresh the renewal calendar, re-audit SaaS spend against Stripe + inbox receipts. Silent unless a new renewal or cancel candidate appeared.", "prompt": "Run the monthly renewals + SaaS sweep. Two skills, chained:

1. Use the track-renewals skill. Re-scan contracts/ and any connected Google Drive, refresh renewals/calendar.md with any newly-added contracts or updated terms.

2. Use the audit-saas-spend skill. Re-aggregate subscriptions from contracts + Stripe + inbox receipts. Compare against last month's saas-audits/ file — flag NEW subscriptions, NEW cancel candidates (tools with growing spend and falling usage signal), and duplicate-in-category additions.

If nothing new has appeared — no new contracts in the next 60d, no new cancel candidates, no duplicates — end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. On the first run (no prior audit exists), always surface — that's the baseline.

Save to saas-audits/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log to outputs.json.", "schedule": "0 8 1 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-data-qa", "name": "Monthly data quality audit", "description": "15th of every month: run DQ checks on the core tables. Catches nulls, duplicate drift, freshness regressions, broken referential integrity. Silent unless a new issue appeared.", "prompt": "Run the monthly data quality audit. Use the analyze skill with subject=data-qa.

Read config/schemas.json for the target tables. For each table: nulls per column (vs expected), duplicates on natural keys, freshness (MAX(updated_at) vs staleness expectation), referential integrity on key joins (foreign-key orphans), cardinality surprises.

Compare against the most recent data-quality-reports/ file so you can flag NEW issues vs. ones already reported. Produce the dated report with pass / warn / fail per check + the SQL used + a suggested fix per fail.

If no NEW failures appeared since the last audit, end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. Surface only when a new fail or warn appeared. On the first run (no prior report exists), always surface — that's the baseline.

Save to data-quality-reports/{YYYY-MM-DD}/report.md and log to outputs.json.", "schedule": "0 8 15 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}]

useCases.title: Draft the operating doc that anchors every other output
useCases.blurb: Company, priorities, rhythm, VIPs, vendor posture, hard nos.
useCases.prompt: Help me set up my operating context.
useCases.fullPrompt: Help me set up my operating context. Use the define-operating-context skill. Interview me briefly on company + priorities + rhythm + key contacts + tools + vendor posture + hard nos + voice, then write the full doc to context/operations-context.md — the source of truth every other skill reads before producing anything substantive.
useCases.description: I interview you briefly and write the full operating doc (company, priorities, rhythm, key contacts, tools, vendor posture, hard nos, voice) to context/operations-context.md. Every other skill reads it first.
useCases.outcome: A locked operating doc at context/operations-context.md. Every skill reads it before producing anything substantive.

useCases.title: The morning brief — what needs me today
useCases.blurb: Inbox + calendar + chat + drive, ranked. One move picked.
useCases.prompt: Give me today's morning brief.
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me today's morning brief. Use the brief skill with mode=daily. Roll up the last 24h from my connected inbox (Gmail / Outlook), calendar (Google Calendar), team chat (Slack), and drive (Google Drive). Produce: Fires (≤3), Today's meetings with prep notes, What changed overnight, Can-wait, and the one move for today. Save to briefs/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I aggregate the last 24h of inbox, calendar, Slack, and drive activity into today's plan: Fires (≤3), today's meetings with prep notes, what changed overnight, and the one move to make.
useCases.outcome: Brief at briefs/{date}.md with the one move called out.

useCases.title: Turn a brain dump into today's plan
useCases.blurb: Parse, bucket, calendar-check, strategic picks.
useCases.prompt: Here's my brain dump — turn this into today's plan.
useCases.fullPrompt: Turn this brain dump into today's plan: {paste thoughts}. Use the brief skill with mode=daily — it'll auto-detect brain-dump sub-mode from the >100-word paste. Bucket into urgent-fires / strategic / operational / future-ideas / personal; calendar reality check; 2-3 strategic picks grounded in my active priorities; delegation candidates; parking lot. Save to briefs/{YYYY-MM-DD}-dump.md.
useCases.description: Paste your unstructured thoughts — I bucket them, cross-reference your calendar, pick 2-3 strategic items against your priorities, and flag delegation candidates.

useCases.title: The Monday operations review
useCases.blurb: What shipped, what's stale, what's next. One move.
useCases.prompt: Give me the Monday operations review.
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me the Monday operations review. Use the run-review skill with period=weekly. Aggregate everything I produced this past week from outputs.json across Planning / Scheduling / Finance / Vendors / Data. Flag what's stale (3+ weeks untouched), gaps against my active priorities, renewals in the next 30 days, and recommend the one most useful move for the week. Save to reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I aggregate everything this agent produced last week from outputs.json, flag gaps against your active priorities and upcoming renewals, and recommend the one move for the week.
useCases.outcome: Review at reviews/{date}.md with the one move called out.

useCases.title: Prep the Q{N} board pack
useCases.blurb: TL;DR, business update, metrics, OKRs, wins, challenges, asks.
useCases.prompt: Prep the Q{N} board pack.
useCases.fullPrompt: Prep the Q{N} board pack. Use the prep-package skill with type=board-pack. Draft the standard 8 sections (TL;DR, business update, metrics, OKRs, wins, challenges, asks, appendix) from my outputs.json + okr-history.json + decisions.json + metrics-daily.json. Flag every TBD. Save to board-packs/{yyyy-qq}/board-pack.md with an optional Google Docs mirror if connected.
useCases.description: Draft the full 8-section board pack (TL;DR, business update, metrics, OKRs, wins, challenges, asks, appendix) from everything this agent has produced. Flags every TBD.
useCases.outcome: Pack at board-packs/{yyyy-qq}/board-pack.md (+ Google Doc mirror if connected).

useCases.title: Draft this month's investor update
useCases.blurb: CEO-voice narrative grounded in OKRs + decisions + metrics.
useCases.prompt: Draft the monthly investor update.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft the monthly investor update. Use the prep-package skill with type=investor-update. CEO-voice narrative ~600-900 words: opening, highlights (3-5), lowlights (1-2), KR status block, asks (2-3), closing. Voice-matched from config/voice.md. Save to investor-updates/{yyyy-qq}/update.md.
useCases.description: CEO-voice narrative grounded in OKR movement, decisions, and metrics. Highlights, honest lowlights, asks, closing — all voice-matched to your samples.

useCases.title: Log a decision with the full context
useCases.blurb: ADR-style: alternatives, trade-offs, rationale, consequences.
useCases.prompt: Log the decision on {topic} — we're going with {X}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Log this decision. Use the log-decision skill. Write an ADR-style record with context, alternatives considered, trade-offs, the decision, rationale, consequences, and links to related initiatives. Save to decisions/{slug}/decision.md and append to decisions.json.
useCases.description: I write an ADR-style decision record with context, alternatives, trade-offs, rationale, and consequences — the thing you'll wish you had written 6 months from now.
useCases.outcome: Record at decisions/{slug}/decision.md and row in decisions.json.

useCases.title: Refresh the OKRs — what's on- and off-track
useCases.blurb: Per-KR value, direction, root cause if off-track.
useCases.prompt: Refresh the OKRs — which KRs are off-track?
useCases.fullPrompt: Refresh the OKRs. Use the track-okr skill. For each KR, pull the current value via any connected OKR tool (Notion / Airtable / Google Sheets), append a snapshot to okr-history.json, classify on-track / at-risk / off-track, and surface root causes from decisions.json and context/operations-context.md priorities for anything off-track. Save to okrs/{yyyy-qq}.md.
useCases.description: I refresh each KR via Notion / Airtable / Google Sheets, classify on-track / at-risk / off-track, and surface root causes from decisions + priorities for anything off-track.
useCases.outcome: Snapshot at okrs/{yyyy-qq}.md and row in okr-history.json.

useCases.title: Find the bottleneck — what's stuck?
useCases.blurb: Clustered evidence + hypothesis + proposed owner.
useCases.prompt: What's stuck — where are we losing time?
useCases.fullPrompt: Tell me what's stuck. Use the identify-bottleneck skill. Cluster evidence from recent weekly reviews, open decisions, open anomalies, off-track OKRs, and bounced follow-ups. Produce 1-3 named bottlenecks each with a hypothesis and a proposed owner to unblock. Append to bottlenecks.json.
useCases.description: I cluster evidence from your reviews, decisions, anomalies, and off-track KRs into named bottlenecks — each with a hypothesis and a proposed owner to unblock.
useCases.outcome: Entries in bottlenecks.json with hypotheses + proposed owners.

useCases.title: Triage my inbox — what needs me today?
useCases.blurb: Needs-me / can-wait / ignore with a verb+object per thread.
useCases.prompt: Triage my inbox.
useCases.fullPrompt: Triage my inbox. Use the triage skill with surface=inbox. Pull last-24h threads via Gmail / Outlook, classify each into needs-me-today / can-wait / ignore, rank the top bucket by time-sensitivity, and state a specific verb+object action per thread (not 'review'). Save to triage/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I classify last-24h threads into needs-me-today / can-wait / ignore, rank the top bucket by time-sensitivity, and write a verb+object action per thread — not 'review.'
useCases.outcome: Triage at triage/{date}.md with counts + top action.

useCases.title: Scan my calendar for the week
useCases.blurb: Overbooks, missing buffers, VIP slots, unprepped meetings.
useCases.prompt: Scan my calendar and find conflicts for the week.
useCases.fullPrompt: Scan my calendar for the next 7 days. Use the triage skill with surface=calendar. Flag overbooks, back-to-back-with-no-buffer, focus-block clashes, unprotected VIP slots, and external meetings without prep. Rank by severity. Save to calendar-scans/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and upsert calendar-conflicts.json.
useCases.description: I scan the next 7 days for overbooks, missing buffers, focus-block clashes, unprotected VIP time, and external meetings with no prep. Ranked by severity.
useCases.outcome: Scan at calendar-scans/{date}.md with the worst conflict called out.

useCases.title: Prep me for my 2pm meeting
useCases.blurb: Attendee intel, agenda, the thing not to forget.
useCases.prompt: Prep me for my 2pm with {name}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Prep me for my upcoming meeting with {name}. Use the brief skill with mode=meeting-pre. Pull recent email threads, public activity, and shared history for each external attendee. Draft a suggested agenda reflecting what they'll likely want, grounded in my priorities from context/operations-context.md. Call out the ONE thing not to forget. Save to meetings/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}-pre.md.
useCases.description: Deep attendee intel (bio, role, prior threads, public activity, shared history) + a suggested agenda + the ONE thing not to forget.

useCases.title: Turn my last call into decisions + follow-ups
useCases.blurb: Decisions, owners, dates, quotes worth keeping.
useCases.prompt: Give me the post-meeting notes from my last call.
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me the post-meeting notes from my last meeting. Use the brief skill with mode=meeting-post. Pull the transcript from my connected Fireflies / Gong (or accept pasted transcript). Extract decisions made, owners + dates for every follow-up, open questions, and 2-4 verbatim quotes worth keeping. Flag any decision-shaped items as log-decision candidates. Save to meetings/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}-post.md.
useCases.description: From your Fireflies / Gong transcript (or paste): decisions made, owners + dates for every follow-up, open questions, and quotes worth keeping.

useCases.title: Book a meeting with someone — 3 times proposed
useCases.blurb: Respects focus blocks. Draft outreach. No surprise events.
useCases.prompt: Book a 30-min meeting with {name} next week.
useCases.fullPrompt: Book a 30-min meeting with {name}. Use the schedule-meeting skill. Propose 3 times that respect my focus blocks, buffers, and max-meetings-per-day. Draft the counterparty message in my voice. Iterate on back-and-forth if needed. Create the event in Google Calendar ONLY after my explicit approval.
useCases.description: I propose 3 times that respect your focus blocks and max-meetings-per-day, draft the outreach in your voice, and create the event only after you approve.
useCases.outcome: Proposal at meetings/{slug}-proposal.md. Event created only after approval.

useCases.title: Plan a trip end-to-end
useCases.blurb: Itinerary draft + flight/hotel criteria + packing list.
useCases.prompt: I'm going to {city} next week — plan the trip.
useCases.fullPrompt: Plan my upcoming trip to {city}. Use the coordinate-travel skill. Read my travel prefs (ask once if missing). Assemble: trip summary, itinerary draft with flight + hotel search criteria, and a destination-adapted packing checklist. Save to trips/{slug}/. Never books — drafts only.
useCases.description: Trip summary + itinerary draft with flight and hotel criteria + a destination-adapted packing checklist. Drafts only, never books.

useCases.title: Draft replies to the inbound emails
useCases.blurb: Your voice, saved as inbox drafts — you send.
useCases.prompt: Draft replies to my needs-me inbox threads.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft replies to my needs-me inbox threads. Use the draft-message skill with type=reply. Pull threads from Gmail / Outlook, draft in my voice (from config/voice.md), save each as a draft in the inbox provider, and write the human-readable record to drafts/reply-{YYYY-MM-DD}-{slug}.md. Never sends.
useCases.description: I pull your needs-me threads, draft replies in your voice from config/voice.md, and save each as a draft in Gmail / Outlook. You approve and send.
useCases.outcome: Drafts at drafts/reply-{date}-{slug}.md + drafts in your inbox.

useCases.title: Track this follow-up so I don't drop it
useCases.blurb: Who / what / by when. Surfaced when due.
useCases.prompt: Track this follow-up: I promised {X} to {name} by {date}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Track this follow-up. Use the draft-message skill with type=followup (TRACK sub-mode). Extract who owes what to whom by when, append to followups.json. When the due date arrives I'll surface it and — when you say 'handle due follow-ups' — draft the fulfillment or an honest bump.
useCases.description: I log the commitment (who / what / by when) to followups.json. On the due date I surface it; when you say 'handle due follow-ups' I draft the fulfillment or an honest bump.

useCases.title: Audit my SaaS spend — what am I paying for?
useCases.blurb: Stripe + contracts + inbox receipts. Cancel candidates.
useCases.prompt: Audit my SaaS spend.
useCases.fullPrompt: Audit my SaaS spend. Use the audit-saas-spend skill. Aggregate subscriptions from my contracts/ folder, connected Stripe, and inbox receipts (Gmail / Outlook). Flag duplicates, unused tools, and rank the top cancel candidates by annual cost × low-usage signal. Save to saas-audits/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: I aggregate subscriptions from your contracts folder, Stripe, and inbox receipts. Flag duplicates, unused tools, and rank top cancel candidates by annual cost × low-usage signal.
useCases.outcome: Audit at saas-audits/{date}.md with ranked cancel candidates.

useCases.title: Build my renewal calendar
useCases.blurb: Renewal dates + notice windows + auto-renew language.
useCases.prompt: Build my renewal calendar for the year.
useCases.fullPrompt: Build my renewal calendar. Use the track-renewals skill. Scan contracts/ and any connected Google Drive folder, extract renewal dates + notice windows + auto-renew language. Maintain the living renewals/calendar.md and a per-quarter digest at renewals/{yyyy-qq}.md.
useCases.description: I scan your contracts folder and Google Drive, extract renewal dates + notice windows + auto-renew language, and maintain a living calendar + per-quarter digest.
useCases.outcome: Living calendar at renewals/calendar.md + per-quarter digest.

useCases.title: Pull the auto-renew clauses from every contract
useCases.blurb: Verbatim quotes + plain-language summary + flags.
useCases.prompt: Pull the auto-renew language from every contract in {folder}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Extract the auto-renew language from every contract in {folder}. Use the extract-contract-clauses skill. Parse each contract, extract the clause with a verbatim quote + plain-language summary + flag if the notice window is onerous or the auto-renew is silent. Also update the renewal calendar.
useCases.description: I parse each contract, extract the clause verbatim + plain-language summary + flag onerous notice windows. Also updates your renewal calendar.
useCases.outcome: Per-contract extracts and an updated renewals/calendar.md.

useCases.title: Draft a renewal-negotiation email
useCases.blurb: Grounded in contract terms + usage + your posture.
useCases.prompt: Draft a renewal-negotiation email for {vendor}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a renewal-negotiation email for {vendor}. Use the draft-message skill with type=vendor (sub-type=renewal). Ground in contract terms from contracts/{vendor}/ and my vendor posture from context/operations-context.md (risk appetite, signature authority, paper preference). Lead with data, specific ask, walk-away. Save as inbox draft + drafts/vendor-renewal-{vendor}.md.
useCases.description: Grounded in the contract terms + your vendor posture: lead with data, specific ask (price, term), clean walk-away. Saved as an inbox draft — you approve and send.

useCases.title: Draft the cancel email for a SaaS
useCases.blurb: Direct, grateful, cites the clause + effective date.
useCases.prompt: Write the cancel email for {SaaS}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Write the cancel email for {SaaS}. Use the draft-message skill with type=vendor (sub-type=cancel). Direct, grateful, cites the cancellation clause + effective date from the contract. Save as an inbox draft + drafts/vendor-cancel-{vendor}.md.
useCases.description: Direct, grateful, and specific — cites the cancellation clause and effective date from the contract. Saved as an inbox draft.

useCases.title: Evaluate a supplier against my criteria
useCases.blurb: Score 1-10, green/yellow/red, concerns, recommendation.
useCases.prompt: Evaluate {supplier} against our criteria.
useCases.fullPrompt: Evaluate {supplier} against our criteria. Use the evaluate-supplier skill. Rubric-based due-diligence. Score 1-10 on each criterion, risk tier green / yellow / red, strengths, concerns, first-call questions, and a clear recommendation. Save to evaluations/{supplier-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Rubric-based due-diligence: score 1-10 per criterion, risk tier green / yellow / red, strengths, concerns, first-call questions, and a recommendation.

useCases.title: Compliance check — is this vendor clean?
useCases.blurb: Frameworks, named officers, incidents. Every claim cited.
useCases.prompt: Run a compliance check on {company}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Run a compliance check on {company}. Use the research-compliance skill. Public-source research via Exa / Perplexity / Firecrawl: frameworks held (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR posture), named officers, recent incidents, litigation. Every claim cited with source URL. Save to compliance-reports/{company-slug}.md.
useCases.description: Public-source research via Exa / Perplexity / Firecrawl: frameworks held, named officers, recent incidents, litigation. Every claim cited with source URL.

useCases.title: Draft trial outreach to a supplier
useCases.blurb: Positioning fit + use case + success criteria + timeline.
useCases.prompt: Reach out to {supplier} for a trial.
useCases.fullPrompt: Reach out to {supplier} for a trial. Use the draft-message skill with type=vendor (sub-type=trial). Write: positioning fit + specific use case + success criteria + honest timeline. Save as an inbox draft + drafts/vendor-trial-{supplier}.md.
useCases.description: Positioning fit + specific use case + success criteria + honest timeline. Saved as an inbox draft.

useCases.title: Reference-check email for a vendor
useCases.blurb: 3-5 targeted questions keyed to what we're evaluating.
useCases.prompt: Draft a reference-check email for {vendor}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Draft a reference-check email for {vendor}. Use the draft-message skill with type=vendor (sub-type=reference-check). 3-5 targeted questions based on what we're evaluating against — not 'tell me about them.' Save as an inbox draft + drafts/vendor-reference-check-{vendor}.md.
useCases.description: 3-5 targeted questions keyed to what we're evaluating — not 'tell me about them.' Saved as an inbox draft.

useCases.title: Score this inbound against my criteria
useCases.blurb: Approve / decline / more-info with evidence per criterion.
useCases.prompt: Score this inbound {press / partnership / advisor} application.
useCases.fullPrompt: Score this inbound application. Use the run-approval-flow skill. Apply the rubric from my context/operations-context.md (priorities + key contacts + hard nos) to produce a scored approve / decline / more-info recommendation with evidence per criterion. Save to approvals/{kind}-{slug}.md.
useCases.description: I apply the rubric from your operating context to produce a scored approve / decline / more-info recommendation with evidence per criterion.

useCases.title: Start tracking a metric
useCases.blurb: Read-only SQL + daily snapshot + registry entry.
useCases.prompt: Start tracking {metric} — watch it.
useCases.fullPrompt: Start tracking {metric}. Use the track-metric skill. Write the read-only SQL against my connected warehouse (Postgres / BigQuery / Snowflake via Composio), snapshot the current value into metrics-daily.json, append the definition to config/metrics.json, and register it for daily cadence.
useCases.description: I write the read-only SQL against your connected warehouse, snapshot the value daily into metrics-daily.json, and register the metric for tracking.
useCases.outcome: Entry in config/metrics.json + first snapshot in metrics-daily.json.

useCases.title: Ask a data question — I'll write the SQL
useCases.blurb: Read-only, cost-warned, saved for reuse.
useCases.prompt: How many signups this week?
useCases.fullPrompt: Answer this data question: {question}. Use the run-sql-query skill. Translate to read-only SQL against my connected warehouse, lazy-introspect any unfamiliar tables into config/schemas.json, warn on cost BEFORE running, execute, save the query for reuse at queries/{slug}/, and return the result with caveats + a run timestamp.
useCases.description: I translate your question to read-only SQL against your connected warehouse, warn on cost before running, execute, save for reuse, and return the result with caveats.

useCases.title: Analyze an experiment — ship / kill / iterate
useCases.blurb: Lift + significance + CI + guardrails. Explicit call.
useCases.prompt: Analyze experiment {name} — how did it do?
useCases.fullPrompt: Analyze experiment {name}. Use the analyze skill with subject=experiment. Pull variant data from my connected warehouse or accept pasted aggregates. Compute lift, statistical significance, 95% CI, observed MDE, guardrail deltas. Make the call: SHIP / KILL / ITERATE / INCONCLUSIVE-EXTEND. Save to analyses/experiment-{slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md. Never recommends SHIP without significance.
useCases.description: Lift + significance + CI + guardrails, with an explicit ship / kill / iterate / inconclusive-extend call. Never recommends SHIP without significance.

useCases.title: Daily anomaly sweep — anything weird?
useCases.blurb: Deviations past baseline + 1-3 hypothesized causes each.
useCases.prompt: Anomaly check — anything weird in the data?
useCases.fullPrompt: Run an anomaly sweep. Use the analyze skill with subject=anomaly. For each metric in config/metrics.json with ≥7 snapshots, compute 7-day and 28-day rolling baselines, flag deviations past the per-metric threshold or default (2σ yellow / 3σ red), and hypothesize 1-3 possible causes from recent decisions, deploys, and experiments. Save to analyses/anomaly-sweep-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and upsert anomalies.json.
useCases.description: I compare every tracked metric against its 7-day and 28-day baselines, flag deviations past threshold, and hypothesize 1-3 causes for each.
useCases.outcome: Sweep at analyses/anomaly-sweep-{date}.md + rows in anomalies.json.

useCases.title: Check data quality on a table
useCases.blurb: Nulls, duplicates, freshness, referential integrity.
useCases.prompt: Run data QA on {table}.
useCases.fullPrompt: Run data QA on {table}. Use the analyze skill with subject=data-qa. Read-only checks: nulls per column, duplicates on natural key, freshness (MAX(updated_at) vs expected staleness), referential integrity on key joins, cardinality surprises. Save to data-quality-reports/{YYYY-MM-DD}/report.md with pass / warn / fail per check and a suggested fix per fail.
useCases.description: Read-only DQ checks on target tables: nulls per column, duplicates on natural keys, freshness, referential integrity, cardinality drift.

useCases.title: Spec a dashboard I can actually build
useCases.blurb: Sections, viz, cadence, read-only SQL per viz.
useCases.prompt: Spec me a growth dashboard.
useCases.fullPrompt: Spec me a {topic} dashboard. Use the spec-dashboard skill. Propose sections, per-section visualizations (what chart, what it shows), cadence (live / daily / weekly / monthly), and the read-only SQL behind each viz. Save the spec to config/dashboards.json — you or your BI tool builds the rendered dashboard.
useCases.description: Sections, per-section visualizations, cadence, and the read-only SQL behind each viz — the spec, not the rendered dashboard.

useCases.title: The weekly metrics rollup
useCases.blurb: Every tracked metric, WoW change, classification, anomalies.
useCases.prompt: Give me the weekly metrics rollup.
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me the weekly metrics rollup. Use the run-review skill with period=metrics-rollup. Read every tracked metric from metrics-daily.json (last 14 snapshots each), compute week-over-week change and classification vs declared direction, flag any open anomaly from anomalies.json, and rank by biggest movement. Save to rollups/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: Cross-metric week-over-week pulse: every tracked metric, WoW change, classification vs direction, open anomalies. Ranked by biggest movement.
useCases.outcome: Rollup at rollups/{date}.md — top 3 movers called out.

useCases.title: Weekly research brief on a topic
useCases.blurb: News + research + social synthesis. Every claim cited.
useCases.prompt: Research brief on {topic} — what's moving?
useCases.fullPrompt: Give me a research brief on {topic}. Use the synthesize-signal skill. News + deep research via Exa / Perplexity + social-feed scan via Firecrawl. Structured brief: what's moving, who's moving it, 3 implications for my company, 3 angles worth acting on. Every claim cited with source URL. Save to signals/{slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md.
useCases.description: News + research + social synthesis via Exa / Perplexity / Firecrawl. Structured brief with what's moving, who's moving it, implications, and angles — every claim cited.
